subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Trail Rangers figure out how many supplies remain after deliveries.
lesson.mini_lesson: Subtract two-digit numbers by place value. Line up tens and ones, subtract ones first, and regroup 1 ten as 10 ones when there are not enough ones.
lesson.objective: Subtract two-digit numbers within 100 using drawings, number lines, and place value.
lesson.concept_explanation: Two-digit subtraction compares ones with ones and tens with tens. When the top number has too few ones, decompose 1 ten into 10 ones.
lesson.common_misconception: A child may subtract the smaller digit from the larger digit instead of regrouping.
lesson.real_world_connection: Subtracting within 100 helps track leftovers, distances, and scores.
lesson.lesson_summary: Subtract ones, regroup if needed, subtract tens, and check the difference.
hints: Focus: Identify the starting number and the amount being taken away.
Strategy: Subtract ones first; when needed, decompose 1 ten into 10 ones without changing the value.
Verify: Add the difference and the amount taken away to recover the starting number.

worked_examples.text: 70 - 30 = 40 because 7 tens minus 3 tens leaves 4 tens.
58 - 24 = 34 because 8 ones - 4 ones = 4 ones and 5 tens - 2 tens = 3 tens.
52 - 28 = 24 because regroup 5 tens 2 ones as 4 tens 12 ones.

hint_ladder: Focus: Identify the starting number and the amount being taken away.
Strategy: Subtract ones first; when needed, decompose 1 ten into 10 ones without changing the value.
Verify: Add the difference and the amount taken away to recover the starting number.

misconception_bank.regrouping_confusion: Learner may forget to trade 1 ten for 10 ones.
misconception_bank.subtracts_smaller_from_larger_digit: Learner may subtract the smaller digit from the larger digit regardless of order.
misconception_bank.tens_ones_alignment_error: Learner may subtract tens from ones or ones from tens.
misconception_bank.count_back_error: Learner may count back too many or too few spaces.

game_theme.title: Subtract Within 100
game_theme.mission_intro: Help the Trail Rangers figure out how many supplies remain after deliveries.

Subtract the ones first. Regroup 1 ten as 10 ones if needed.
Then subtract the tens and write the difference.

level_banks.label: Level 1: Subtract Tens
level_banks.focus: Subtract multiples of ten using tens rods.

hints: Focus: Start with 80. Keep its tens and ones aligned while you take away 30.
Strategy: Subtract by place: 0 - 0 = 0 ones, and 8 - 3 = 5 tens.
Verify: Add the difference to 30; it should rebuild the starting amount, 80.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: No regrouping is needed because 0 ones can remove 0 ones. Subtract ones: 0 - 0 = 0; subtract tens: 8 - 3 = 5. This gives 50. Why it works: decomposing changes the form of 80, not its value, so equal place values can be subtracted. Check: 50 + 30 = 80.

question_audio.text: Subtract 80 minus 30. What is the difference? Base-ten blocks show the starting amount 80 as 8 tens and 0 ones, with directions to take away 30; the difference is 50.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: Base-ten blocks show the starting amount 80 as 8 tens and 0 ones, with directions to take away 30; the difference is 50.
questions.accessible_description: Base-ten blocks show the starting amount 80 as 8 tens and 0 ones, with directions to take away 30; the difference is 50.

hints: Focus: Start with 49. Keep its tens and ones aligned while you take away 17.
Strategy: Subtract by place: 9 - 7 = 2 ones, and 4 - 1 = 3 tens.
Verify: Add the difference to 17; it should rebuild the starting amount, 49.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: No regrouping is needed because 9 ones can remove 7 ones. Subtract ones: 9 - 7 = 2; subtract tens: 4 - 1 = 3. This gives 32. Why it works: decomposing changes the form of 49, not its value, so equal place values can be subtracted. Check: 32 + 17 = 49.

question_audio.text: Subtract 49 minus 17. What is the difference? A subtraction model shows a starting quantity of 49, a crossed-out quantity of 17, and asks for the remaining difference, 32.

level_banks.label: Level 4: Subtract With Regrouping
level_banks.focus: Decompose 1 ten into 10 ones before subtracting.

hints: Focus: Start with 52. Keep its tens and ones aligned while you take away 28.
Strategy: Decompose 1 ten: 5 tens 2 ones becomes 4 tens 12 ones. Subtract 8 ones, then 2 tens.
Verify: Add the difference to 28; it should rebuild the starting amount, 52.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: The ones place has 2, fewer than 8, so decompose 1 ten to make 12 ones. Then 12 - 8 = 4 ones and 4 - 2 = 2 tens. This gives 24. Why it works: decomposing changes the form of 52, not its value, so equal place values can be subtracted. Check: 24 + 28 = 52.

question_audio.text: Subtract 52 minus 28. What is the difference? Base-ten blocks show the starting amount 52 as 5 tens and 2 ones, with directions to take away 28; the difference is 24.
question_audio.label: Read Question

question_audio.text: Rangers counted 81 animal tracks. They marked 56 on a map. How many tracks are not marked yet? Base-ten blocks show the starting amount 81 as 8 tens and 1 ones, with directions to take away 56; the difference is 25.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: Base-ten blocks show the starting amount 81 as 8 tens and 1 ones, with directions to take away 56; the difference is 25.
questions.accessible_description: Base-ten blocks show the starting amount 81 as 8 tens and 1 ones, with directions to take away 56; the difference is 25.

questions.prompt: A class prepared 60 seed packets and planted 27. How many packets are left?
hints: Focus: Start with 60. Keep its tens and ones aligned while you take away 27.
Strategy: Decompose 1 ten: 6 tens 0 ones becomes 5 tens 10 ones. Subtract 7 ones, then 2 tens.
Verify: Add the difference to 27; it should rebuild the starting amount, 60.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model and try the place-value strategy again.

questions.explanation: The ones place has 0, fewer than 7, so decompose 1 ten to make 10 ones. Then 10 - 7 = 3 ones and 5 - 2 = 3 tens. This gives 33. Why it works: decomposing changes the form of 60, not its value, so equal place values can be subtracted. Check: 33 + 27 = 60.

question_audio.text: A class prepared 60 seed packets and planted 27. How many packets are left? A place-value chart organizes the starting amount 60 into 6 tens and 0 ones for subtracting 27; the difference is 33.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: A place-value chart organizes the starting amount 60 into 6 tens and 0 ones for subtracting 27; the difference is 33.
questions.accessible_description: A place-value chart organizes the starting amount 60 into 6 tens and 0 ones for subtracting 27; the difference is 33.

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting a math mission about Subtract Within 100. Look at the story and think about what the characters need to solve. Math missions are easier when you name the numbers and the model first. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Subtract Within 100. Look at the subtraction model and connect each part to the math words. Use the vocabulary on the page to explain your thinking. Remember to check place value, units, or symbols before you answer. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Subtract Within 100. Follow the model one step at a time. Say what each number means before you look at the final answer. If a symbol appears, read it in words like plus, minus, equals, greater than, or less than. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo helps you try Subtract Within 100 with support. Build or study the model first, then connect it to the question. A good strategy is to explain the answer in math words before choosing. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
